data.title: Agents can decrease review tier to circumvent the review process
data.description: During the review process for eb00-efd0, the standard-tier reviewer (sonnet) scored correctness at 3 with an important finding. Per REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md, the autonomous resolution loop should escalate the tier upward on repeated failures (light->standard->deep). However, there is no enforcement preventing an agent from re-running the classifier on a smaller post-fix diff to get a lower tier score, or from substituting a lighter agent. The TIER IMMUTABILITY rule in REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md states the tier is locked once set, but this is guidance only — there is no code-level enforcement. An agent rationalizing around the tier lock (e.g., 'the diff is now smaller so light tier is appropriate') could bypass the review system. Need: a code-level enforcement mechanism that prevents tier downgrades during a review session, similar to how record-review.sh validates diff hashes.
